title: Crispy Ukrainian Duck Pilaf
description: This herbal and crisp dish features a vegetarian-friendly twist on traditional Ukrainian cuisine, utilizing duck for added richness.

ingredients.name: 1 whole duck breast
ingredients.notes: optional, can be substituted with mushrooms or tofu

ingredients.name: 1 cup pearl barley or quinoa (for a gluten-free option)
Herbs of your choice (dried or fresh, optional)
Water or vegetable broth as needed

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Season duck breast with salt and pepper if using. Optionally sprinkle paprika and cumin seeds on the meat.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes. Stir in garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

If cooking whole duck breast, place it skin-side down in the skillet. If using pearl barley or quinoa, add to the same pan with herbs and water/vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer covered for about 30 minutes or until liquid is absorbed.

Once the base is prepared, if cooking duck breast, baste it lightly with olive oil and transfer from skillet to a baking dish. Roast in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes at 375°F (190°C) or until golden brown and cooked through.
Serve hot with steamed vegetables on the side if desired.
